task: Debug emergency close kill chain for Trade #3433 GBP_JPY SELL
local_output: Trade was killed 1 second after entry due to margin emergency override
opus_correction: Root cause: emergency_threat = 85 from margin check bypasses grace cap because it runs before the else-block containing grace logic. Fix: manual trades during grace period now override margin emergency, logging the concern but capping threat at YELLOW (60).
opus_reasoning: Traced execution path: two 100K positions on $9.8K account -> margin > 80% -> emergency_threat = 85 -> line 597 sets threat before grace cap at line 632 (in else block) -> BLACK zone -> instant close. The else-block ordering meant emergency always bypassed grace. Fix preserves safety logging while respecting manual trade intent.

local_output: Generated trade stats and pipeline health report
opus_correction: P0: finding_id NULL in 100% of trades blocks condition-level analysis. P1: GBP_JPY cooldown gate not enforcing (7 trades, 59min avg gap vs 2h required). Win rate improving but avg loss 2.7x avg win creates negative expectancy.
opus_reasoning: Systematic pipeline audit: trade DB query -> per-pair aggregation -> regression detection (finding_id) -> churning pattern (GBP_JPY frequency) -> risk:reward inversion calculation -> OANDA/flight/vault/scout health checks.
